title: Gemba — generalized Kanban-style web UI for any work tracker × any agent orchestrator
description:
Root epic. Holds the twelve locked architectural decisions. Do NOT close until every phase epic closes.

# Project charter

Gemba is a single-binary Go service with an embedded React SPA that pairs exactly one WorkPlane adaptor (work tracker) with exactly one OrchestrationPlane adaptor (agent runtime) and renders whatever the two declare.

Beads + Gas Town are the v1 reference adaptors. Jira (WorkPlane) + LangGraph (OrchestrationPlane) are v1 forcing-function adaptors that prove the contract isn't accidentally Beads-shaped or Gas-Town-shaped.

# Twelve locked architectural decisions

Changes to any of these require an escalation, not a local edit.

1. TOPOLOGY. Standalone sidecar binary (`gemba`). Separate repo, separate process. Talks to one configured WorkPlaneAdaptor and one configured OrchestrationPlaneAdaptor. Default config discovers what a workspace can satisfy.
2. BACKEND. Go single binary. `go:embed` ships the SPA. Cobra CLI. chi routing. SSE for live updates.
3. FRONTEND. React + TypeScript + Vite. shadcn/ui + Tailwind. TanStack Query/Table. @dnd-kit. React Flow. cmdk.
4. ADAPTOR-AGNOSTIC UI. No role/pack vocabulary in the SPA outside `web/src/extensions/<adaptor-id>/` capability-gated widgets.
5. PLUGGABLE WORKSPACES. `Workspace.kind ∈ worktree | container | k8s_pod | vm | exec | subprocess`, declared by the orchestration adaptor.
6. MULTI-WORKSPACE, NOT FEDERATED. Multiple workspaces in a single Gemba install in v1. Identity carries WorkspaceID; labels mark fed:safe / fed:bridge / fed:blocked.
7. MUTATION MODEL. Server-enforced X-GEMBA-Confirm nonce. `--dangerously-skip-permissions` (verbatim from Claude Code) disables for the session.
8. AUTH. Localhost by default. Non-loopback bind without `--auth` is a startup error. Token: 256-bit, argon2id, printed once. TLS via certs or `--tls-self-signed`. OIDC stubbed for v1.1.
9. DATA INTEGRITY. Never write any backend's private storage directly. Every mutation through the adaptor's public CLI/API.
10. DECLARATIVE UX. UI shows `OrchestrationPlaneAdaptor.declared_state()` (desired) vs `observed_state()` (actual) with drift highlighted. Adaptors without a declarative source render implicit-desired from the capability manifest.
11. ZFC FOR THE UI. Present data, surface actions; the operator decides. No decision logic in TypeScript.
12. DISTRIBUTION. Homebrew tap, npm wrapper, GitHub Releases for macOS / Linux / Windows / FreeBSD.

# Cross-cutting primitives every adaptor sees

`WorkItem`, `AgentRef`, `Relationship`, `Evidence`, `DefinitionOfDone` (informational-only), `Sprint` + `TokenBudget`, `CostMeter`, `EscalationRequest`, `CapabilityManifest`. See `domain.md` for the full type system.

# Out of scope for v1

- Cross-workspace federation (deferred; fed:* labels prevent precluding it)
- DoD enforcement of any kind (informational-only)
- Per-capability permission scoping (all-or-nothing)
- Multi-transport adaptors (exactly one of `api | jsonl | mcp` per adaptor)
- Cross-repo WorkItems
- Mobile native apps; Confluence-like docs surface
- Direct private-storage writes against any backend (ever)
- Becoming an in-tree feature of any backend (always sidecar)

# Definition of Done

- All 14 phase epics closed
- Conformance suite green on all four v1 adaptors (Beads, Gas Town, Jira, LangGraph)
- At least two transports proven end-to-end (`api` + one of `jsonl | mcp`)
- Sprint + TokenBudget live with three-tier inform/warn/stop enforcement
- Binary published for all target platforms
- `brew install gemba` works
- `gemba doctor` passes clean against any reference adaptor pair
- Documentation site live; "Writing a Gemba adaptor" guide published
- No backend-specific vocabulary appears in SPA outside `web/src/extensions/<adaptor-id>/`

title: Phase 1: Foundation — repo, skeleton binary, CI, embed pipeline
description:
Ship a binary that serves a "hello" SPA via go:embed, installs cleanly on all target platforms, and has a green CI pipeline. No features — just the scaffolding every later phase depends on.

# Definition of Done
- `go install github.com/MikeBengtson/gemba/cmd/gemba@latest` works
- `gemba serve` binds :7666, serves embedded SPA, closes cleanly on SIGTERM
- `make dev` runs Vite HMR + Go hot reload with working proxy
- `make build` produces a single binary with the SPA embedded
- GitHub Actions: lint, vet, test, build matrix for all platforms
- Release workflow exists (dry-run tested) but not yet cutting releases

title: Bootstrap Go module with layout matching gvid conventions
description:
# Goal
Initialize the Go module and directory layout. Mirror gvid's structure where possible to keep diff-readability across the gastown-tooling ecosystem high.

# Outputs
- `go.mod` at module root `github.com/MikeBengtson/gemba`
- `cmd/gemba/main.go` — Cobra root, calls `internal/cli`
- `internal/cli/` — Cobra subcommands (`serve`, `doctor`, `version`)
- `internal/server/` — chi router + handlers
- `internal/core/` — adaptor-agnostic types (placeholder; gm-e3.1 fills this)
- `internal/adapter/` — adaptor implementations (placeholder; phase 6+ fills these)
- `internal/transport/` — `api`/`jsonl`/`mcp` shims (placeholder; gm-e3.4 fills this)
- `web/` — Vite + React + TS scaffold (gm-e1.4 fills this)
- `Makefile`, `.golangci.yml`, `.gitignore`

# Definition of Done
- `go build ./...` succeeds
- `go vet ./...` clean
- `golangci-lint run` clean

**Resolves DDs:** —

title: Cobra CLI skeleton: `gemba serve`, `gemba doctor`, `gemba version`
description:
# Goal
Wire the three v1 commands. `serve` is the long-running server; `doctor` is a one-shot diagnostic; `version` prints semver + commit.

# Outputs
- `gemba serve [--listen ADDR] [--auth FILE] [--tls-cert/--tls-key] [--tls-self-signed] [--dangerously-skip-permissions] [--config PATH]`
- `gemba doctor` — prints which WorkPlane and OrchestrationPlane adaptors the current workspace can satisfy; exits non-zero if no pair detected
- `gemba version`

# Definition of Done
- All three commands present, --help text accurate
- `serve` parses flags, doesn't yet route requests (that's gm-e4.1)
- `doctor` enumerates registered adaptors and reports detection status
- Bind policy is enforced at startup (rejects non-loopback without `--auth`); covered by test

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 8

title: go:embed the SPA dist directory, serve from binary
description:
# Goal
Embed the built SPA in the Go binary so `gemba serve` ships zero-runtime-dependency.

# Outputs
- `internal/server/spa.go` — `//go:embed web/dist`
- SPA fallback handler routes unknown paths to `index.html` (SPA routing) BUT preserves API 404s on `/api/*` paths (see gm-b2)
- `make build` produces a single binary; `file bin/gemba` confirms no external SPA dir needed

# Definition of Done
- Binary built without `web/dist` present fails fast with a clear error
- Binary built with `web/dist` serves SPA + falls back correctly
- API 404s on `/api/*` are NOT shadowed by the SPA fallback (regression test for gm-b2)

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 2

title: Scaffold Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui
description:
# Goal
Initialize the SPA toolchain. No application logic yet — just the empty shell.

# Outputs
- `web/package.json` with Vite 6, React 18, TypeScript 5, Tailwind 3, shadcn/ui CLI installed
- `web/vite.config.ts` with proxy to `localhost:7666` for `/api`, `/events`
- `web/src/main.tsx` boot
- `web/src/App.tsx` — placeholder "Gemba" landing
- `web/tailwind.config.js`, `web/components.json` (shadcn)

# Definition of Done
- `cd web && npm install && npm run dev` starts Vite on :5173, proxies API to :7666
- `npm run build` produces `web/dist/` under 500KB gzipped (placeholder budget)
- TypeScript strict mode enabled, no `any` in scaffold

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 3

title: Makefile: `make dev`, `make build`, `make release`
description:
# Goal
Single-command developer experience.

# Outputs
- `make dev` — runs Vite + Go server concurrently with hot reload (uses `air` or equivalent)
- `make build` — builds SPA, embeds, builds Go binary into `bin/gemba`
- `make release` — calls `goreleaser release --snapshot` for local testing
- `make test` — `go test -race ./...` + `cd web && npm test`
- `make lint` — `golangci-lint run` + `cd web && npm run lint`

# Definition of Done
- All targets work from a fresh clone
- Documented in README

**Resolves DDs:** —

title: GitHub Actions CI: lint, test, build matrix
description:
# Goal
Green CI on every push and PR.

# Outputs
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- Matrix: macOS-14, ubuntu-22.04, windows-2022 × Go 1.23, 1.24
- Steps: setup-go, setup-node, `make lint`, `make test`, `make build`
- Caching for Go modules and npm
- Status badge in README

# Definition of Done
- CI green on `main`
- Failure on `golangci-lint`, `go vet`, `go test -race`, or `npm test` blocks merge

**Resolves DDs:** —

title: Phase 2: Validation gates — socialize the four highest-risk DDs before contracts go to code
description:
Sociopolitical validation phase. Before the core contracts (gm-e3) freeze, the four highest-risk design decisions go to external maintainers and prospective users for explicit feedback. Each gate exits GREEN (decision confirmed) or FALLBACK-DOCUMENTED (decision adjusted with the rationale recorded in `domain.md`).

This phase is intentionally before code so that a contract rejected by Beads, Jira, Gas Town, Gas City, or LangGraph maintainers does not become a sunk cost.

# Definition of Done
- All four gates GREEN or FALLBACK-DOCUMENTED
- A short "Design Decisions" page lives in `docs/dd/` summarizing each outcome
- gm-e3 (core contracts) unblocked

title: Gate V1 — DD-9: socialize 3-core edge taxonomy with Beads author
description:
# Goal
Confirm that the three core edge types (`blocks`, `parent_child`, `relates_to`) plus adaptor-declared extensions is a contract Beads can write against without losing fidelity. Beads's seven native edges become 3 core + 4 Beads-namespaced extensions.

# Inputs
- domain.md DD-9 + edge-mapping table for Beads
- Phase 1 evidence in landscape.md

# Outputs
- A short write-up posted to the Beads repo discussions
- Outcome (GREEN | FALLBACK) recorded in `docs/dd/dd-09.md`

# Definition of Done
- Beads author has acknowledged in writing (or 14d non-response → proceed with documented assumption)
- If FALLBACK, the alternative shape is specified and reflected in domain.md before gm-e3 starts

**Resolves DDs:** DD-9

title: Gate V2 — DD-1: socialize AgentRef shape with Gas Town, Gas City, and one external orchestrator
description:
# Goal
Confirm `AgentRef{agent_id, display_name, role, parent_id?, agent_kind}` is enough surface for orchestrators to render without amputating dimensions, and that work-tracker adaptors (Beads, Jira, Linear) can synthesize the missing pieces from labels/custom fields.

# Inputs
- domain.md DD-1 + federation table (Beads claim, Jira custom field, Linear bot user, GitHub Projects assignee)
- landscape.md gap "no standard for agent as first-class work-tracker citizen"

# Outputs
- Discussions opened in Gas Town, Gas City, and at least one of {LangGraph, OpenHands, CrewAI}
- Outcome recorded in `docs/dd/dd-01.md`

# Definition of Done
- Three orchestrator and two tracker maintainers have responded (or 14d non-response → documented assumption)
- If FALLBACK, the alternative shape is reflected in domain.md before gm-e3 starts

**Resolves DDs:** DD-1

title: Gate V3 — DD-4 + DD-14: socialize CostMeter axes and Sprint-as-token-budget semantics
description:
# Goal
Confirm two things:
(a) CostMeter axes (`tokens_total`, `dollars_total`, `wallclock_seconds`) at six rollup scopes is the right surface for orchestrators that have native cost telemetry to export.
(b) Sprint-as-token-budget with three-tier `inform | warn | stop` enforcement at epic and sprint scope is a UX that doesn't break Scrum-fluent users in unproductive ways.

# Inputs
- domain.md DD-4, DD-14
- At least two Scrum-fluent prospective users for (b)
- At least one non-Gas-Town adaptor-implementer candidate for (a)

# Outputs
- Discussions / interviews completed
- Outcome recorded in `docs/dd/dd-04-14.md`

# Definition of Done
- (a) Two adaptor-implementer candidates have responded
- (b) Two Scrum-fluent users have walked through a click-through prototype and given feedback in writing
- If FALLBACK on either, the alternative is reflected in domain.md before gm-e3 starts

**Resolves DDs:** DD-4, DD-14

title: Gate V4 — DD-13: socialize evidence-synthesis stance with first two adaptor authors
description:
# Goal
Confirm that synthesizing Evidence from git/CI for adaptors without native evidence (and tagging `synthesized: true`) is acceptable to maintainers of trackers that do have native evidence (Linear PR-link auto-detection, GitHub Projects native PR↔Issue links).

# Inputs
- domain.md DD-13
- Reference WorkPlane adaptors (Beads + Jira) under design

# Outputs
- Outcome recorded in `docs/dd/dd-13.md`
- A reusable evidence-synthesis library design (consumed by gm-e11.6)

# Definition of Done
- Two adaptor authors have signed off on the synthesis approach
- If FALLBACK, the alternative is reflected in domain.md before gm-e3 starts

**Resolves DDs:** DD-13

title: Phase 3: Core contracts — domain types, adaptor interfaces, conformance harness
description:
Define the adaptor-agnostic surface in code: WorkPlane and OrchestrationPlane interfaces, capability manifests, registration & version negotiation, conformance test harness, event schema, OTEL trace propagation, noop reference adaptors. After this phase the adaptor work (gm-e6 onward) can begin in parallel.

# Definition of Done
- Interfaces compile cleanly in Go and codegen valid TS types
- Conformance harness runs A–F groups against a noop adaptor and reports green
- Adaptor registration + describe RPC works across all three transports (api, jsonl, mcp)
- Capability manifest types are codegenned into `web/src/types/`

**Resolves DDs:** DD-2, DD-5, DD-7, DD-9, DD-10, DD-12, DD-14, DD-15

title: Define core types: WorkItem, AgentRef, Relationship, Evidence, DefinitionOfDone, Sprint, TokenBudget
description:
# Goal
Specify the adaptor-agnostic domain types in Go and emit matching TypeScript via codegen.

# Outputs
- `internal/core/types.go` — all core types per `domain.md` §2
- `internal/core/state.go` — `StateCategory ∈ Backlog|Unstarted|Started|Completed|Canceled`
- `internal/core/dod.go` — informational-only DoD: `{acceptance_criteria, notes, version}`
- `internal/core/budget.go` — Sprint, TokenBudget, three-tier thresholds
- TS codegen target writes `web/src/types/core.gen.ts`

# Definition of Done
- All types have godoc; round-trip through JSON tested
- TS types match Go shape; tsc-clean

**Resolves DDs:** DD-2, DD-10, DD-14

title: Define WorkPlaneAdaptor interface + WorkPlane CapabilityManifest
description:
# Goal
Specify the WorkPlane interface that every work-tracker adaptor implements.

# Outputs
- `internal/core/workplane.go` — interface per `domain.md` §3
- `CapabilityManifest` carrying `transport ∈ api|jsonl|mcp`, `state_map`, `edge_extensions`, `field_extensions`, `relationship_extensions`, capability booleans (`sprint_native`, `token_budget_enforced`, `evidence_synthesis_required`)
- Sprint operations (`list_sprints`, `read_budget_rollup`)

# Definition of Done
- Interface compiles
- Documented in `docs/adaptors/workplane.md`

**Resolves DDs:** DD-9, DD-12, DD-14, DD-15

title: Define OrchestrationPlaneAdaptor interface + OrchestrationCapabilityManifest
description:
# Goal
Specify the OrchestrationPlane interface every agent-runtime adaptor implements.

# Outputs
- `internal/core/orchestration.go` — interface per `domain.md` §3
- `OrchestrationCapabilityManifest` carrying `transport`, `workspace_kinds[]`, `group_modes[]` (`static|pool|graph`), `cost_axes[]`, `escalation_kinds[]`, `peek_modes[]`
- `declared_state()` / `observed_state()` return `WorkspaceTopology`

# Definition of Done
- Interface compiles
- Documented in `docs/adaptors/orchestration.md`

**Resolves DDs:** DD-5, DD-7, DD-10, DD-12

title: Adaptor registration + version negotiation across api / jsonl / mcp transports
description:
# Goal
Common registration protocol so any adaptor (regardless of transport) can `describe()` and bind to the core.

# Outputs
- `internal/transport/api/` — HTTP+JSON adaptor host
- `internal/transport/jsonl/` — newline-delimited JSON over stdio adaptor host
- `internal/transport/mcp/` — Model Context Protocol adaptor host (recommended-not-required)
- `gemba adaptor register --transport <kind> --target <addr>` CLI
- Version negotiation: core advertises `core_version`, adaptor advertises `adaptor_version` + `protocol_version`; mismatch → clear error

# Definition of Done
- All three transport hosts can register the noop adaptor (gm-e3.7)
- Version mismatches surface as actionable errors

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12, DD-15

title: Conformance test harness: `gemba adaptor test` runs groups A–F
description:
# Goal
Mechanical proof that an adaptor satisfies the contract.

# Outputs
- `gemba adaptor test --target <addr> --transport <kind>`
- Group A: describe + capability shape
- Group B: WorkItem CRUD round-trip (or session lifecycle for orchestration)
- Group C: edge / relationship round-trip (WorkPlane only)
- Group D: subscribe / event delivery
- Group E: capability-declared optional features (sprint, budget, escalation, evidence)
- Group F: error semantics (idempotency, retry, transport disconnect)

# Definition of Done
- Harness runs against the noop adaptor and reports green
- Per-group pass/fail summary; CI integration optional via `--junit` output

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12, DD-15

title: Event schema + OTEL trace propagation across the adaptor boundary
description:
# Goal
Adaptor-emitted events flow through the core to the SSE hub with W3C trace context preserved.

# Outputs
- `internal/events/schema.go` — unified `GembaEvent` union (workitem.*, session.*, escalation.*, budget.inform/warn/stop, capability.refresh)
- OTEL exporter wrapper; adaptors inherit `CallCtx.trace_id`
- Prometheus-style `/metrics` endpoint exposes `workitem.state_duration_seconds`, `assignment.total_cost_dollars`, `escalation.open_count`, `session.spawn_rate`, `workspace.acquire_latency_seconds`

# Definition of Done
- Events flow adaptor → hub → SPA with `trace_id` intact (verified via OTEL collector stub)
- Budget events fire on first threshold crossing per epic/sprint scope

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12, DD-14

title: Noop in-memory reference adaptors (WorkPlane + OrchestrationPlane) passing conformance
description:
# Goal
In-memory reference implementations used to exercise the UI without a real backend and to validate conformance harness.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/noop/workplane.go` — in-memory store, jsonl transport
- `internal/adapter/noop/orchestration.go` — in-memory agents/groups/sessions, jsonl transport
- Both ship with `gemba serve --noop` shortcut

# Definition of Done
- Both pass conformance groups A–F
- `gemba serve --noop` boots a usable UI in under 2 seconds

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12, DD-15

title: Phase 4: Transport — HTTP API, OpenAPI/TS codegen, SSE hub, mutation nonce
description:
The wire layer between the SPA and the core. Adaptor-agnostic — it serves whatever the registered adaptors expose, gated by capability manifests.

# Definition of Done
- `/api/openapi.json` published; SPA TS client codegenned
- `/events` SSE endpoint streams the unified `GembaEvent` schema with capability filtering
- Every mutation requires `X-GEMBA-Confirm` nonce; replay rejected
- `--dangerously-skip-permissions` disables nonce gating for the session and emits a startup WARN

title: HTTP API layer with chi router
description:
# Goal
Wire chi as the HTTP router; mount adaptor-fronting handlers under `/api/v1/`.

# Outputs
- `internal/server/router.go` — chi mount
- `/api/v1/workitems`, `/agents`, `/groups`, `/workspaces`, `/sessions`, `/escalations`, `/sprints`, `/capabilities`
- Standard middleware: request ID, logging, recovery, CORS off-by-default

# Definition of Done
- Smoke tests exercise each route against the noop adaptor pair (gm-e3.7)

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 2

title: OpenAPI spec + TypeScript client codegen
description:
# Goal
Single source of truth for the wire schema; SPA consumes generated types and a typed client.

# Outputs
- `/api/openapi.json` served at runtime; static copy in `docs/api/`
- `internal/server/openapi.go` builds spec from chi routes + struct tags
- `web/src/api/gen/` — codegenned client + types
- `make codegen` runs before `make build`

# Definition of Done
- Generated client compiles tsc-clean
- Schema lints with Spectral

**Resolves DDs:** —

title: SSE hub with capability-filtered subscriptions
description:
# Goal
Live updates from adaptors fan out to subscribed SPA clients with capability filtering.

# Outputs
- `internal/events/hub.go` — fan-out broker; per-client filter based on capability manifests
- `/events?topics=...` SSE endpoint
- Backpressure: slow clients dropped after a configurable lag budget
- Heartbeat every 15s; reconnect-with-Last-Event-ID support

# Definition of Done
- Load test: 1000 concurrent SSE clients sustained for 5 min without leak
- A subscription that asks for capabilities the active adaptor does not declare gets a clear 4xx

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12, locked decision 2

title: Mutation confirmation gating with X-GEMBA-Confirm nonce
description:
# Goal
Every mutation requires a server-issued, single-use nonce.

# Outputs
- `POST /api/v1/confirm` — issues a 256-bit nonce with TTL (default 60s)
- Mutation handlers reject without `X-GEMBA-Confirm: <nonce>`; nonce is single-use, server-tracked
- `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag disables enforcement; startup logs a WARN
- `YOLO_MODE=1` env var equivalent

# Definition of Done
- Tests: missing nonce → 400; reused nonce → 400; expired nonce → 400; valid → 200
- Replay attack test passes
- `--dangerously-skip-permissions` disables and logs as expected

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 7

title: Phase 5: Auth — bind policy, token, TLS, OIDC stub
description:
Auth layer. Localhost by default; explicit gates for remote bind.

# Definition of Done
- Bind policy verifiably rejects 0.0.0.0 without `--auth`
- Token auth: 256-bit, argon2id-hashed at rest, printed once on first run
- TLS: user certs OR `--tls-self-signed` with 1-year SANs and SHA-256 fingerprint print
- OIDC adapter: interface present, mock provider works, real providers v1.1

title: Bind policy: localhost-only default, explicit gate for remote
description:
# Goal
Refuse to start with a non-loopback bind unless `--auth` is provided.

# Outputs
- Startup validation runs before chi mount
- Clear error: "non-loopback bind requires --auth; got --listen 0.0.0.0:7666"
- Loopback (127.0.0.1, [::1], localhost) bypasses the gate

# Definition of Done
- Test exhaustively covers bind matrix × auth matrix
- See gm-b3 (token auth must not be silently disabled on loopback)

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 8

title: Token auth: generate, store hashed, verify via header or cookie
description:
# Goal
First-run generates a 256-bit token, prints it ONCE, stores argon2id hash at `~/.gemba/tokens/primary`.

# Outputs
- `gemba auth token rotate` rotates and re-prints
- Bearer header `Authorization: Bearer <token>` accepted
- Signed session cookie (HMAC-SHA256) accepted; cookie issued by `POST /api/auth/login` exchanging the bearer
- All token storage hashed at rest

# Definition of Done
- Token never logged anywhere
- Constant-time compare in verify path
- Tests cover rotate-while-running

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 8

title: TLS: user certs OR --tls-self-signed
description:
# Goal
Two TLS modes: user-provided cert/key pair, or generate a self-signed cert.

# Outputs
- `--tls-cert PATH --tls-key PATH` mode
- `--tls-self-signed` mode generates a 1-year cert with SANs for localhost + bind IP, prints SHA-256 fingerprint at startup
- Cert/key permissions checked (warn if world-readable)

# Definition of Done
- Both modes work end-to-end
- `--tls-self-signed` regenerates on missing or expired cert

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 8

title: OIDC auth adapter (stubbed for v1, shipped in v1.1)
description:
# Goal
Interface present so v1.1 can ship real providers without API churn.

# Outputs
- `internal/auth/oidc/` — interface + mock provider for tests
- `--auth oidc --oidc-config PATH` flag wired but errors with "not implemented in v1" unless `MOCK_OIDC=1`

# Definition of Done
- Mock provider exchanges code → token in tests
- Real GitHub + Google providers tracked as v1.1 follow-ups (not part of this bead)

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 8

title: Phase 6: Reference WorkPlane — Beads adaptor
description:
First conformance-passing WorkPlane adaptor. Beads is chosen as reference because it has the richest native model (seven edge types, native sprints, native evidence-tracking via Dolt) — proving the contract works against Beads gives confidence it will simplify cleanly for Jira/Linear/etc.

Transport: `api` (HTTP+JSON shim wrapping `bd --json`).

# Definition of Done
- All conformance groups A–F green
- Beads sees no API surface change (adaptor lives entirely in `internal/adapter/beads/`)
- A regression suite exercises every Beads operation Gemba relies on

title: Beads WorkPlane adaptor: CRUD + query via `bd --json`
description:
# Goal
Wrap `bd create / update / list / show / dep add` behind the WorkPlaneAdaptor interface.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/beads/workplane.go`
- `internal/adapter/beads/types.go` — Beads-specific shapes that mirror `bd` JSON output
- Mapping: `Bead` → `WorkItem` (id, title, description, type, priority, status → state_category mapping, labels, dependencies)

# Definition of Done
- Conformance group B (CRUD) green
- Round-trip preserves all Beads-specific fields via the extension channel

**Resolves DDs:** DD-2

title: Beads edge mapping: 7 native edges → 3 core + 4 extensions
description:
# Goal
`blocks` and `parent_child` are core; `relates_to` is core. Beads-native `discovered_from`, `waits_for`, `replies_to`, `conditional_blocks` are declared as extensions in the manifest under `edge_extensions`.

# Outputs
- Edge mapping table in `internal/adapter/beads/edges.go`
- Conformance group C green

# Definition of Done
- Round-trip: a bead with all 7 edge types loaded through Gemba and written back has identical edges
- The dep graph UI renders core edges always, extensions only when declared

**Resolves DDs:** DD-9

title: Beads AgentRef ↔ bd claim federation
description:
# Goal
`AgentRef` synthesized from `bd update <id> --claim <agent-id>` data. Role/parent_id derived from labels (`agent:role:*`, `agent:parent:*`) where present; null otherwise.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/beads/agents.go`
- Mapping documented in `docs/adaptors/beads.md`

# Definition of Done
- Listing assignees on a bead returns AgentRef[] with role/parent populated when labels are present
- Round-trip: setting an agent on a WorkItem in Gemba updates the right `--claim` + labels in Beads

**Resolves DDs:** DD-1

title: Beads DoD pass-through: free-text acceptance_criteria field
description:
# Goal
Map `DefinitionOfDone.acceptance_criteria` + `notes` to a delimited section in the bead's description (since Beads has no native DoD field). Pass-through only — no enforcement.

# Outputs
- Description section delimiters: `<!--gemba:dod-->...<!--/gemba:dod-->`
- Read parses; write replaces; round-trip preserves anything outside the delimiters

# Definition of Done
- Round-trip preserves both `acceptance_criteria` and `notes`
- Beads users editing the description outside Gemba do not lose their DoD

**Resolves DDs:** DD-3

title: Beads evidence synthesis from work-history + Dolt refs
description:
# Goal
Synthesize Evidence[] from `bd work-history <id>` (commits, completion-events, dolt commits referenced).

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/beads/evidence.go` — uses the shared synthesis library (gm-e11.6)
- All synthesized evidence tagged `synthesized: true`
- Native Beads-tracked references (`evidence:` labels) NOT marked synthesized

# Definition of Done
- A bead with merged PRs returns Evidence with PR URLs + commit SHAs
- Conformance group E (capability-declared evidence) green

**Resolves DDs:** DD-13

title: Beads subscribe/event delivery via fsnotify + `bd events`
description:
# Goal
Live event stream from Beads to the SSE hub.

# Outputs
- fsnotify watcher on `.beads/`
- Subscribe to `bd events --json` if available, else fall back to polling `bd list --since`
- Coalescing: bursts collapsed to one update per WorkItem per 100ms

# Definition of Done
- Conformance group D green
- A `bd update` made outside Gemba surfaces in the SPA within 500ms

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12

title: Beads adaptor passes conformance suite groups A–F
description:
# Goal
Run `gemba adaptor test --target <local-bd> --transport api` and report all groups green. Capture the report in `docs/adaptors/beads-conformance.md`.

# Definition of Done
- All groups green
- Report committed to docs

**Resolves DDs:** DD-2, DD-9, DD-12, DD-13

title: Phase 7: Reference OrchestrationPlane — Gas Town adaptor
description:
First conformance-passing OrchestrationPlane adaptor. Gas Town picked as reference because it's the most mature multi-agent orchestrator with stable v1 semantics, fixed-role pack, and tmux-grounded session model.

Transport: `api` (HTTP+JSON shim wrapping `gt --json`).

# Definition of Done
- All conformance groups A–F green
- Gas Town sees no API surface change (adaptor lives in `internal/adapter/gastown/`)
- Mail view (gm-e12.x) gated behind Gas Town capability declaration; not in core

title: Gas Town: list_agents, list_groups (convoy → static, pool → pool)
description:
# Goal
Enumerate Gas Town agents and groups behind the OrchestrationPlaneAdaptor interface.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/gastown/agents.go`
- Convoy → `AgentGroup{mode: 'static'}`
- Polecat pool → `AgentGroup{mode: 'pool'}`
- Role taxonomy (Mayor, Deacon, Witness, Refinery, Polecats) preserved via `AgentGroup.extensions['role']`

# Definition of Done
- Conformance group B (agents/groups CRUD) green
- The pack-agnostic UI renders Gas Town's groups without role-name switch statements

**Resolves DDs:** DD-7

title: Gas Town: acquire_workspace / release_workspace (tmux rig → WorkspaceKind=worktree)
description:
# Goal
Map Gas Town's rig (git worktree + tmux session) to `Workspace{kind: worktree, fs_scoped: true}`.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/gastown/workspace.go`
- `acquire_workspace` calls `gt rig add` (idempotent); `release_workspace` calls `gt rig stop`
- Capability manifest declares `workspace_kinds: [worktree]`

# Definition of Done
- Conformance group B (workspace lifecycle) green
- Idempotent acquire (existing rig is reused, not recreated)

**Resolves DDs:** DD-5

title: Gas Town: session lifecycle + tmux capture-pane peek
description:
# Goal
Map Gas Town tmux sessions to `Session` lifecycle (start, pause, resume, end, peek).

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/gastown/sessions.go`
- `start_session` invokes `gt session start <agent>`
- `peek_session` returns last N lines from `tmux capture-pane`
- `pause_session` / `resume_session` use the gt session-pause primitives where present

# Definition of Done
- Peek returns within 200ms for a typical session
- Conformance group B (sessions) green

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12

title: Gas Town: cost meter synthesis (tokens + wallclock from session telemetry)
description:
# Goal
Synthesize `CostMeter` from Gas Town's session telemetry + Claude Code transcripts.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/gastown/cost.go` — uses shared library where possible
- `tokens_total` from transcripts; `wallclock_seconds` from session start/end; `dollars_total` derived if pricing config present
- Capability manifest declares `cost_axes: [tokens, wallclock]` plus `dollars` when pricing is configured

# Definition of Done
- Per-session and per-WorkItem rollups within ±5% of ground truth
- Falls back gracefully when transcripts are missing

**Resolves DDs:** DD-4

title: Gas Town: escalation mapping (`gt escalate` → EscalationRequest)
description:
# Goal
Map Gas Town's `gt escalate` outputs to `EscalationRequest{source: 'orchestrator_pause'}`.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/gastown/escalations.go`
- Watch the escalation stream (mail-based today); convert each to an EscalationRequest event
- Capability manifest declares `escalation_kinds: [orchestrator_pause, hitl_interrupt]`

# Definition of Done
- A `gt escalate -s HIGH` produces an EscalationRequest visible in the SPA escalations inbox within 1s

**Resolves DDs:** DD-6

title: Gas Town adaptor declares transport: api (HTTP+JSON shim around gt --json)
description:
# Goal
Adaptor manifests with `transport: 'api'`. No MCP bridge required (MCP is recommended-not-required per DD-15).

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/gastown/transport.go` — thin HTTP+JSON shim
- `gemba adaptor register --transport api --target <addr>` works
- Documentation example in `docs/adaptors/gastown.md`

# Definition of Done
- Adaptor registers, describes, and serves all conformance traffic over `api` transport

**Resolves DDs:** DD-8, DD-15

title: Gas Town adaptor passes conformance suite groups A–F
description:
# Goal
Run `gemba adaptor test` against a local Gas Town install. Report green. Capture in `docs/adaptors/gastown-conformance.md`.

# Definition of Done
- All groups green
- Report committed

**Resolves DDs:** DD-5, DD-6, DD-7, DD-12, DD-15

title: Phase 8: Forcing-function WorkPlane — Jira adaptor
description:
Prove the WorkPlane abstraction by implementing Jira: the hardest common-case tracker (workflow FSM, custom fields, rate limits, no native agent concept, no native evidence). If the contract handles Jira's quirks, Linear / Plane / Shortcut are easy.

Transport: `api` (Atlassian REST v3).

# Definition of Done
- All conformance groups A–F green against an Atlassian Jira Cloud sandbox
- Round-trip read/write smoke test merged in CI

title: Jira WorkPlane adaptor: CRUD + query via Atlassian REST v3
description:
# Goal
Wrap Atlassian Cloud REST v3 issue endpoints behind the WorkPlaneAdaptor interface.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/jira/workplane.go`
- Field mapping: Jira issue → WorkItem (key as id, summary as title, description, issuetype, priority, status → state_category)
- JQL → `WorkItemQuery`
- Token-based auth via Atlassian API token

# Definition of Done
- Conformance group B (CRUD) green against a sandbox

**Resolves DDs:** DD-2

title: Jira: workflow FSM → state_map.valid_transitions with refresh
description:
# Goal
Each Jira project has its own workflow. Adaptor reads `/rest/api/3/project/{key}/statuses` and `/issue/{key}/transitions`, exposes them as `state_map` in the capability manifest.

# Outputs
- Per-project state_map cached with 5-minute TTL; manual refresh available
- `transition()` validates against `valid_transitions[from]` and rejects illegal moves with a clear error

# Definition of Done
- A Jira project with a non-trivial workflow renders correct columns and only-legal Kanban moves

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12

title: Jira: synthesize Agent concept via custom fields (gemba.agent_role, gemba.parent_agent)
description:
# Goal
Jira has no native agent concept. Synthesize AgentRef from two custom fields: `gemba.agent_role` (string) and `gemba.parent_agent` (string).

# Outputs
- Adaptor probes for the fields on first connect; if missing, prints exact `jira-cli` commands to create them, then errors out with actionable guidance
- Mapping: `assignee.accountId` → `AgentRef.agent_id`; custom fields → role / parent_id

# Definition of Done
- A Jira issue with the custom fields populated returns a fully-shaped AgentRef
- Issues without the fields return AgentRef with role/parent null

**Resolves DDs:** DD-1

title: Jira: DoD pass-through (acceptance_criteria custom field, fallback delimited section in description)
description:
# Goal
If the project has an "Acceptance Criteria" custom field, use it. Else fall back to a delimited section in the description identical to the Beads adaptor approach (gm-e6.4).

# Outputs
- Probe behavior matches gm-e8.3 (probe + clear guidance)
- Round-trip preserves DoD content; informational-only

# Definition of Done
- Both modes work end-to-end against the sandbox

**Resolves DDs:** DD-3

title: Jira: rate-limit-aware subscribe via webhooks + coalescing
description:
# Goal
Jira has aggressive rate limits and no streaming API. Use webhooks for change events; coalesce bursts; fall back to polling with `updated > <last>` if webhooks aren't available.

# Outputs
- Webhook receiver mounted at `/webhooks/jira` (HMAC-verified)
- Coalescing: per-issue updates collapsed to one event per 200ms
- Polling fallback respects 429s with exponential backoff + jitter

# Definition of Done
- A Jira issue updated externally surfaces in the SPA within 1s of the webhook
- Polling fallback survives a 429 spike without dropping events

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12

title: Jira adaptor passes conformance suite groups A–F
description:
# Goal
Run `gemba adaptor test` against the Atlassian Cloud sandbox. Report green. Capture in `docs/adaptors/jira-conformance.md`.

# Definition of Done
- All groups green
- Report committed

**Resolves DDs:** DD-1, DD-2, DD-3, DD-9, DD-12

title: Phase 9: Forcing-function OrchestrationPlane — LangGraph adaptor
description:
Prove the OrchestrationPlane abstraction by implementing LangGraph: the hardest common-case orchestrator (no native agent identity, no native work item, graph-mode grouping, checkpoint-based session). If the contract handles LangGraph's impedance mismatch, OpenHands / Devin / Factory are easy.

Transport: `mcp` — the second-transport requirement for DD-15 transport plurality.

# Definition of Done
- All conformance groups A–F green
- End-to-end demo: a LangGraph thread checkpointed and resumed from the Gemba UI

title: LangGraph: Agent synthesis from node paths, thread_id as session
description:
# Goal
LangGraph has no native agent concept. Synthesize `AgentRef` from node paths in the compiled graph; map `thread_id` to `Session`.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/langgraph/agents.go`
- Each terminal node in the compiled graph → an AgentRef (configurable via the manifest)

# Definition of Done
- A simple graph with three nodes returns three AgentRefs
- A running thread is observable as a Session

**Resolves DDs:** DD-1

title: LangGraph: AgentGroup{mode:'graph'} surfacing
description:
# Goal
Surface the compiled graph as `AgentGroup{mode: 'graph'}`; members resolved on read; topology rendered opaque in v1 (per DD-7).

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/langgraph/groups.go`
- Capability manifest declares `group_modes: [graph]`

# Definition of Done
- A LangGraph application registers as one or more graph-mode AgentGroups
- The UI renders graph-mode groups without trying to lay out the topology

**Resolves DDs:** DD-7

title: LangGraph: checkpoint → suspend/resume; HITL interrupt → EscalationRequest
description:
# Goal
Map LangGraph checkpointer state to Session suspend/resume semantics; HITL interrupt becomes an `EscalationRequest{source: 'hitl_interrupt'}`.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/langgraph/sessions.go`
- `pause_session` writes a checkpoint; `resume_session` invokes from the latest checkpoint

# Definition of Done
- End-to-end: pause from UI, modify state externally, resume — verified working

**Resolves DDs:** DD-6

title: LangGraph: cost meter from LangSmith token pull
description:
# Goal
Pull token usage from LangSmith if configured; map to `CostMeter.tokens_total`.

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/langgraph/cost.go`
- Capability manifest declares `cost_axes: [tokens]` (plus `dollars` if LangSmith pricing data is present)

# Definition of Done
- A graph that ran with LangSmith tracing returns a populated CostMeter

**Resolves DDs:** DD-4

title: LangGraph: workspace declared `exec` with documented fs_scoped caveat
description:
# Goal
LangGraph has no workspace primitive. Declare `Workspace.kind: 'exec'`, `fs_scoped: true with caveat` (the caveat: graphs sharing a process share fs).

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/langgraph/workspace.go`
- Capability manifest declares `workspace_kinds: [exec]`
- Documented caveat in `docs/adaptors/langgraph.md`

# Definition of Done
- The Workspace.kind switch in the UI renders the exec-style affordance set

**Resolves DDs:** DD-5

title: LangGraph adaptor passes conformance suite (graph-opaque group tests)
description:
# Goal
Run `gemba adaptor test --transport mcp` against a LangGraph reference deployment. Report green. Capture in `docs/adaptors/langgraph-conformance.md`.

# Definition of Done
- All groups green
- Report committed
- This adaptor is the proof for DD-15 transport plurality (`mcp` end-to-end)

**Resolves DDs:** DD-1, DD-4, DD-5, DD-6, DD-7, DD-15

title: Phase 10: Stub OrchestrationPlane — Gas City adaptor
description:
Designed-for, primary at GC GA. Keeps the surface area Gas-City-compatible without depending on Gas City being shipped.

# Definition of Done (v1)
- Stub compiles
- `gemba doctor` detects a Gas City workspace and routes to this adaptor
- Capability manifest declares "alpha/stub"
- All RFC follow-up questions tracked

title: Gas City adaptor skeleton mirroring Gas Town shape
description:
# Goal
Skeleton compiles; methods return `ErrNotImplementedAlpha` with a link to the RFC follow-up bead (gm-e10.3).

# Outputs
- `internal/adapter/gascity/orchestration.go`
- Same interface compliance as gm-e7 but every method gates on a stub check

# Definition of Done
- Adaptor registers and describes itself as `alpha/stub`
- No method panics; all return clean errors

title: Gas City workspace detection (city.toml → gascity adaptor)
description:
# Goal
`gemba doctor` finds `city.toml` and routes to the Gas City adaptor.

# Outputs
- Detection logic in `internal/cli/doctor.go`
- Manifest declares `workspace_kinds: [container, k8s_pod, vm]` per Gas City roadmap

# Definition of Done
- A directory with `city.toml` is detected as a Gas City workspace

title: Gas City RFC follow-up: publish spec questions, collect answers
description:
# Goal
Publish the open spec questions from `domain.md` Gas City section. Collect answers from Gas City maintainers. Wire up real adaptor surface as answers land.

# Outputs
- `docs/adaptors/gascity-rfc.md` with questions
- Tracking issues opened in the Gas City repo
- Updates to the adaptor as answers come in

# Definition of Done
- All open questions either answered or marked "post-GC GA" with rationale

title: Phase 11: Cross-cutting features — DoD, CostMeter, Escalation, Capability, Sprint+Budget, Evidence, Transport plurality
description:
The cross-cutting primitives that ride on top of two real adaptor pairs. Each child wires a primitive end-to-end (adaptor → core → SSE → SPA).

# Definition of Done
- DoD informational-only schema published
- CostMeter rollups render in the insights panel
- EscalationRequest pipeline wired across the SPA (card badge, inbox, board banner)
- Capability negotiation visibly hides controls per active manifest
- Sprint + TokenBudget live with three-tier inform/warn/stop enforcement
- Shared evidence-synthesis library used by Beads + Jira
- At least two transports proven end-to-end (api + one of jsonl/mcp)

title: Document informational-only DoD stance + ship minimal schema
description:
# Goal
Publish the v1 DoD stance: informational, no enforcement, free-text + version. Ship the schema and the Beads/Jira pass-through behavior.

# Outputs
- `docs/dd/dd-03.md`
- `internal/core/dod.go` shipped
- Adaptor pass-through tested

# Definition of Done
- A WorkItem with DoD round-trips through Beads and Jira intact

**Resolves DDs:** DD-3

title: CostMeter rollups + insights panel surface
description:
# Goal
Aggregate CostMeter at six scopes (assignment, work item, agent, group, epic, sprint) and surface in the insights panel.

# Outputs
- `internal/core/cost_rollup.go`
- Insights panel charts (in gm-e12.x) read from this surface

# Definition of Done
- A workspace with two adaptors emitting cost data shows correct rollups within ±1%

**Resolves DDs:** DD-4

title: EscalationRequest pipeline: card badge, /escalations inbox, board banner
description:
# Goal
End-to-end escalation surface: adaptor emits → core stores → SSE broadcasts → SPA shows badge + inbox + banner. One-click ack.

# Outputs
- `internal/core/escalations.go`
- Card badge appears on the WorkItem associated with the escalation
- `/escalations` inbox lists open escalations
- Kanban banner appears when any escalation is open in the board's filter scope

# Definition of Done
- An escalation emitted by an adaptor surfaces in all three places within 1s
- Ack roundtrips through the adaptor

**Resolves DDs:** DD-6

title: Capability-negotiation UI layer: hide controls by manifest; field extensions; transport-aware routing
description:
# Goal
The SPA reads `CapabilityManifest` and dynamically:
- Hides columns / controls / panels the active manifests don't declare
- Renders adaptor-namespaced extension widgets where extension fields/edges exist
- Routes calls to the right transport hosts

# Outputs
- `web/src/capabilities/` — manifest reader, gate components
- `<Capability has="sprint_native">...</Capability>` JSX gate

# Definition of Done
- Snapshot test (gm-e12.x) catches any backend-vocabulary leak

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12, DD-15

title: Sprint + TokenBudget: entity, rollup engine, three-tier inform/warn/stop enforcement, UI gauges
description:
# Goal
Sprint as a token-budget container. Three-tier `inform | warn | stop` thresholds per epic and per sprint. Tokens are the only enforced axis in v1; dollars and wallclock render but do not gate.

# Outputs
- `internal/core/sprint.go`, `internal/core/budget.go` shipped from gm-e3.1
- Rollup engine consumes CostMeter from all sources (gm-e11.2)
- Threshold-crossing emits `budget.inform`, `budget.warn`, `budget.stop` events (gm-e3.6)
- `stop` threshold prevents new `start_session` calls until lifted
- SPA gauges render at sprint and epic scope

# Definition of Done
- A sprint with a token budget below current spend correctly blocks new sessions
- All three thresholds surface in the SPA

**Resolves DDs:** DD-14

title: Shared evidence-synthesis library (git log, GitHub PR API, CI status)
description:
# Goal
A reusable package that any adaptor can call to synthesize Evidence from PRs, commits, and CI runs.

# Outputs
- `internal/core/evidence_synth/` — git log walker, GitHub PR resolver, CI status puller
- Used by Beads adaptor (gm-e6.5), Jira adaptor (where applicable), other future adaptors
- All synthesized evidence tagged `synthesized: true`

# Definition of Done
- Library covers git, GitHub, and at least one CI provider
- Beads + Jira adaptors both consume it

**Resolves DDs:** DD-13

title: Transport plurality harness: prove at least two transports end-to-end (api + one of jsonl/mcp)
description:
# Goal
Demonstrate that the conformance suite passes identically across two transports for the same logical adaptor.

# Outputs
- Test matrix wires the noop adaptor on `api` and `jsonl`; LangGraph adaptor (gm-e9) on `mcp`
- Conformance report compares results across transports
- CI matrix includes the multi-transport run

# Definition of Done
- All conformance groups green on both transports for the noop adaptor
- LangGraph (`mcp`) green per gm-e9.6

**Resolves DDs:** DD-15

title: Phase 12: UI — adaptor-agnostic SPA
description:
The full SPA. Every component reads from typed core APIs and the active CapabilityManifest. No backend-specific vocabulary outside `web/src/extensions/<adaptor-id>/`.

# Definition of Done
- Snapshot test catches any backend-vocabulary leak in the core SPA
- All three workflows (plan/refine, day-of ops, retro/release) demonstrably work
- Extensions for Beads + Gas Town live under `web/src/extensions/{beads,gastown}/`

title: App shell: sidebar, topbar, routing, theme
description:
# Goal
The chrome around every view. Theme respects system dark mode; sidebar nav surfaces top-level destinations.

# Outputs
- `web/src/App.tsx` shell + `<Outlet/>` routing via TanStack Router
- Sidebar items: Board, Backlog, Graph, Insights, Escalations, Capability Browser, Mail (gated)
- Topbar: workspace selector, command palette trigger, user menu

# Definition of Done
- Routes deep-link cleanly
- Theme toggles persist

title: react-query data layer with SSE-driven invalidation
description:
# Goal
TanStack Query is the data layer; SSE events trigger targeted query invalidation per capability filter.

# Outputs
- `web/src/data/` — query keys, fetchers
- `web/src/data/sse.ts` — SSE consumer; routes events to invalidations
- Filters subscribe by capability so a workspace without sprints doesn't subscribe to budget events

# Definition of Done
- A WorkItem updated externally appears in the SPA within 500ms

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12

title: WorkItem grid (Jira/Linear-style) with @tanstack/react-table, virtualized to 10k rows
description:
# Goal
Power-user view: 10k rows, virtualized, column presets, saved filters, JSONL bulk import.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Grid.tsx`
- Columns derive from `CapabilityManifest.field_extensions` + always-on core columns
- Saved filters persisted to `localStorage`; shareable via URL hash

# Definition of Done
- 10k rows render at 60fps on a 2024 MacBook
- JSONL import dry-run + commit

title: Agents dashboard with live tiles
description:
# Goal
Per-agent tile with live status, current session, recent activity. Agent-detail drawer on click.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Agents.tsx`
- Reads `AgentRef[]` from the OrchestrationPlane adaptor
- Tile renders `agent_kind` (agent vs human) with distinct visual treatment

# Definition of Done
- Tile updates within 500ms of an agent state change

title: WorkItem detail drawer with tabs
description:
# Goal
Description, labels, edges, activity, evidence, DoD, sprint+budget context. Tabs for each.

# Outputs
- `web/src/components/WorkItemDrawer.tsx`
- Edge tab renders core edges always + extension edges only if declared
- Evidence tab marks synthesized items distinctly
- DoD tab shows informational-only banner

# Definition of Done
- Opens from grid, board, palette, deep link

title: Global cmdk command palette
description:
# Goal
Cmd-K opens the palette. Searches WorkItems, agents, groups, escalations. Keyboard-first navigation.

# Outputs
- `web/src/components/Palette.tsx` (cmdk)
- Action surface: navigate, create WorkItem, dispatch session, ack escalation

# Definition of Done
- Palette opens in <50ms; search results stream in

title: AgentGroup board (Kanban) with @dnd-kit — the home screen
description:
# Goal
Kanban-style board with columns derived from `state_category`, names from `CapabilityManifest.state_map`. Drag-drop round-trips through `WorkPlaneAdaptor.transition`.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Board.tsx`
- Swimlanes by group/assignee/label
- WIP limits per column
- Filter chips: priority, tier, agent, label, fed:*

# Definition of Done
- Drag a card from Backlog to Started: nonce-gated transition fires; success rolls forward, failure rolls back

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 7

title: AgentGroup board view (mode: static | pool | graph)
description:
# Goal
Render any AgentGroup mode declared by the active OrchestrationPlane.

# Outputs
- `web/src/components/AgentGroupBoard.tsx`
- `static` → fixed-member tiles; `pool` → desired-vs-actual gauge + check log; `graph` → opaque box with member list

# Definition of Done
- All three modes render correctly against the noop adaptor and Gas Town

**Resolves DDs:** DD-7

title: Backlog board across all workspaces
description:
# Goal
Cross-workspace backlog view. Filterable by workspace, label, sprint, agent.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Backlog.tsx`
- Columns: Backlog (state_category) only; rows grouped by epic / sprint

# Definition of Done
- Renders 5k items at 60fps

title: WorkItem creation flow with smart defaults
description:
# Goal
Single-shot WorkItem creation with adaptor-aware defaults (priority, type, labels) and a preview of the resulting CRUD payload.

# Outputs
- `web/src/components/WorkItemCreate.tsx`
- Defaults seeded from current view context (board column → state, swimlane → group, etc.)

# Definition of Done
- Creation works against Beads + Jira + noop without changes

title: Bulk actions in grid view
description:
# Goal
Multi-select rows in the grid; apply transitions / labels / assignments / sprint moves in bulk. Each action nonce-gated.

# Outputs
- `web/src/components/BulkActions.tsx`
- Per-action preview before commit

# Definition of Done
- Bulk transition of 100 items succeeds end-to-end against the noop adaptor

title: YOLO mode toggle in UI (gated by server flag)
description:
# Goal
Surface `--dangerously-skip-permissions` state in the UI with a persistent banner.

# Outputs
- Topbar pill rendered when YOLO is on
- Banner appears on every mutation surface

# Definition of Done
- Banner cannot be dismissed by the client; state read from server

title: Desired-vs-actual view (drift dashboard)
description:
# Goal
Render `OrchestrationPlaneAdaptor.declared_state()` vs `observed_state()` with drift highlighted.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Drift.tsx`
- Tints column headers in the board view when drift exists
- Standalone page with per-difference detail

# Definition of Done
- A workspace with manually-induced drift renders the difference

**Resolves DDs:** DD-10

title: Adaptor capability browser
description:
# Goal
Enumerate registered adaptors and inspect their capability manifests.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Capabilities.tsx`
- Per-adaptor detail: transport, declared capabilities, state_map, edge_extensions, field_extensions, conformance status

# Definition of Done
- Renders correctly for all v1 adaptors

title: Provider-aware agent detail view (Workspace.kind switch)
description:
# Goal
Agent detail surface chooses an affordance set based on the current `Workspace.kind`.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/AgentDetail.tsx`
- `worktree` → Attach button; `k8s_pod` → Peek + pod status; `subprocess` → process tree; `exec` → last command + exit code; `container` / `vm` → equivalents

# Definition of Done
- Each kind renders the correct affordance set against a sample workspace

**Resolves DDs:** DD-5

title: Dependency graph with React Flow
description:
# Goal
Render the WorkItem dep graph. Three core edge types always; extension edges only if declared.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Graph.tsx`
- Cycle highlighting; critical-path mode; node click opens drawer

# Definition of Done
- 1000-node graph renders interactively

title: Insights panel with OTEL-driven charts
description:
# Goal
Sprint burn-down, spawn rate, completion rate, stuck-session minutes, token spend, escalation backlog.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Insights.tsx`
- Charts read from `/api/v1/metrics` (OTEL + CostMeter rollups)

# Definition of Done
- All charts render against a workspace with synthetic data

title: Phase 13: Molecules — formula catalog + progress view
description:
Render multi-step workflow DAGs as first-class UI objects. Capability-gated — only adaptor pairs that declare `formulas_native` (Gas Town does) get this surface.

# Definition of Done
- Capability-gated view appears for Gas Town workspaces
- Step-by-step replay of prompts/outputs available

**Resolves DDs:** DD-12

title: Formula catalog view
description:
# Goal
List installed formulas with description, step count, recent invocations.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Formulas.tsx` (capability-gated)

# Definition of Done
- Renders for Gas Town; absent for other adaptors

title: Molecule progress view with step timeline
description:
# Goal
For each running molecule, render the step DAG with status; per-step prompt + output replay; checkpoint state.

# Outputs
- `web/src/views/Molecule.tsx`

# Definition of Done
- Live progress updates within 1s
- Replay of a completed molecule reproduces the original prompts/outputs

title: Phase 14: Release — distribution, docs, announcement
description:
Cut v1.0. Ship for all platforms; publish docs; announce.

# Definition of Done
- Binary on Homebrew tap, npm, GitHub Releases for macOS / Linux / Windows / FreeBSD
- Docs site live with quickstart, adaptor authoring guide, migration guide
- Announcement posted

title: goreleaser configuration for all platforms
description:
# Goal
Single command cuts a release across all targets.

# Outputs
- `.goreleaser.yml`
- Targets: macOS (Intel + arm64), Linux (amd64 + arm64), Windows (amd64), FreeBSD (amd64)
- Signed releases (cosign or Apple notarization for macOS)

# Definition of Done
- `goreleaser release --snapshot` succeeds
- A real tag-driven release succeeds end-to-end

title: Homebrew tap
description:
# Goal
`brew install YOUR_ORG/tap/gemba` works.

# Outputs
- Tap repo with formula
- Auto-bump on goreleaser publish

# Definition of Done
- Fresh install verifies on macOS Intel and Apple Silicon

title: npm wrapper package: `npm install -g gemba`
description:
# Goal
Cross-platform install via npm; the wrapper downloads the matching binary.

# Outputs
- `npm/gemba` package
- Postinstall downloads + verifies SHA

# Definition of Done
- Fresh install verifies on all four target OSes

title: Docs site (Docusaurus or Astro Starlight)
description:
# Goal
Public docs at `gemba.dev` (or chosen domain). Quickstart, configuration, every CLI flag, every API endpoint, every label, every capability flag.

# Outputs
- `docs/` source
- Built site deployed via Cloudflare Pages or GitHub Pages
- Search works (Algolia DocSearch or local)

# Definition of Done
- Quickstart works for a new user
- All CLI flags + API endpoints documented
- Dark theme default

title: Public 'Writing a Gemba adaptor' guide
description:
# Goal
Tutorial that walks an adaptor author from `cobra add` to a passing conformance suite.

# Outputs
- `docs/guides/writing-an-adaptor.md`
- Worked example: a minimal in-memory WorkPlane adaptor

# Definition of Done
- An external developer follows the guide end-to-end and produces a conformance-passing adaptor

title: Announcement: HN, Reddit, Discord, blog post
description:
# Goal
Release announcement.

# Outputs
- Blog post on `gemba.dev`
- HN submission, Reddit /r/programming + /r/MachineLearning
- Discord post in the gastown community + adjacent communities (LangChain, Atlassian developer)

# Definition of Done
- Posted; comments monitored for the first 48h

title: BUG: handle adaptor-degraded state gracefully
description:
# Goal
When any adaptor (WorkPlane or OrchestrationPlane) is degraded — daemon offline, network unreachable, rate-limited — the SPA shows a clear banner and keeps non-affected views functional.

# Outputs
- Health-probe endpoint per adaptor
- Banner component reads probe state; surfaces actionable error
- Mutations against a degraded adaptor surface a clear "adaptor degraded; mutation rejected" error rather than hanging

# Definition of Done
- Killing the bd daemon mid-session produces a clear banner; readonly views keep working from cache; mutations error cleanly
- Same for Gas Town, Jira, LangGraph

**Resolves DDs:** DD-13

title: BUG: SPA fallback must not shadow API 404s
description:
# Goal
The SPA fallback handler routes unknown paths to `index.html` for client-side routing — but `/api/*` and `/events*` 404s must surface as 404s, not silent SPA renders.

# Definition of Done
- Test: `GET /api/v1/nonexistent` returns 404 with `application/json` error body
- Test: `GET /not-a-real-page` returns 200 with the SPA `index.html`

title: BUG: token auth must not be silently disabled on a loopback bind
description:
# Goal
If the user passes `--auth token` AND `--listen 127.0.0.1`, token auth must still be enforced. Don't silently drop auth just because the bind is loopback.

# Definition of Done
- Test: `--listen 127.0.0.1 --auth token` requires the bearer header on every request
- Test: missing bearer returns 401, not 200

**Resolves DDs:** locked decision 8
